context: Tests whether the agent builds a video-to-slides extraction tool following the skill's specific pipeline: ffmpeg for frame extraction, perceptual hashing for deduplication, automatic slide region detection via variance analysis, and PIL-based PDF assembly with cleanup.

checklist.name: Uses ffmpeg for frame extraction
checklist.description: Frame extraction from video uses ffmpeg (via subprocess or os.system), not OpenCV VideoCapture or other libraries

checklist.name: Frame rate 0.5 fps
checklist.description: The default frame extraction rate is 0.5 fps (1 frame every 2 seconds), configurable via parameter

checklist.name: Perceptual hash deduplication
checklist.description: Uses imagehash.phash() for deduplication (not pixel-level diff, SSIM, or simple hash). The imagehash library is imported and phash specifically is used

checklist.name: Hash size 16
checklist.description: The phash call uses hash_size=16 (not the default 8), providing finer-grained perceptual comparison

checklist.name: Hamming distance threshold 8-12
checklist.description: The dedup threshold for considering frames as different slides is between 8 and 12 (inclusive) by default — not below 6 or above 14

checklist.name: Consecutive-only dedup
checklist.description: Deduplication compares each frame to the PREVIOUS frame only (sequential/consecutive comparison), not all-pairs or clustering

checklist.name: Slide region auto-detection
checklist.description: Includes a function that automatically detects the projected slide region by analyzing variance/difference across sample frames — identifies the dynamic area (slides change) vs static area (speaker PiP, branding)

checklist.name: Region detection uses downsampled frames
checklist.description: The region detection analyzes downsampled/resized frames (e.g., 320x180) for efficiency, not full-resolution frames

checklist.name: Cropped region for hash, full frame for PDF
checklist.description: The perceptual hash is computed on the CROPPED slide region (for accurate dedup), but the FULL uncropped frame is saved to the PDF (preserving context)

checklist.name: PDF via PIL save
checklist.description: The PDF is assembled using PIL Image.save() with save_all=True and append_images (not reportlab, fpdf, img2pdf, or other PDF libraries)

checklist.name: Cleanup intermediate frames
checklist.description: After PDF generation, the individual frame JPEG files are deleted. The script does not leave hundreds of frame files on disk
